highlights: Hub latest: 999c149 data: evening roster test standup complete

cro.description: Growth strategy, monetization. Gazelle-intense focus on revenue.

cmo.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, Thursday night smoke test. Quick context: last standup we aligned on App Store scope — full build, all four tabs, ship what we have. CEO deferred the Supabase unification. Tonight's question is simple: what can each of you actually finish in the next 30 minutes? 716 commits in seven days tells me we're building fast. Let's make sure we're building toward the finish line. Go around the horn — Elon, you're up. Elon (CTO): Two uncommitted files sitting in the Hub. That's sloppy — takes me three minutes to commit those, so I'll knock that out immediately. Beyond that, the three critical sync gaps from A-001 are still open — the podcast MDX mismatch, the blog post rendering, and the App Store submission blocker. I can run a diagnostic on all three tonight and produce a concrete fix plan with line-level diffs. The build is healthy, 7 TODOs in the codebase, nothing blocking.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Revenue reality check: we have zero active revenue streams. Zero subscribers. The single biggest unlock is getting Lever into the App Store — that's the gate to everything. My 30-minute task tonight is mapping the pre-launch monetization sequence: what happens the day the app goes live, what's the free tier, when does a paid tier show up, what's the conversion trigger. I need that ready so we're not scrambling post-approval. The idle cash at $75,340 is already tracked under J-001, CEO said keep it liquid, I'm not revisiting that tonight.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: I've got 7 faith lessons across 8 dimensions — CEO wants 15 to 20. Tonight I can draft 3 new lessons, targeting the dimensions that only have one lesson each. That gets us to 10 and builds momentum toward the target. Breadth-first, like we agreed. I'll also flag that Jett's vaccine is tomorrow — A-006 is tracked, just making sure Joshua sees it before morning.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The App Store submission isn't a technical problem, it's a presentation problem. Screenshots, description copy, the first impression. Tonight I'll audit what we have for store listing assets and identify exactly what's missing. If we don't have polished screenshots for all four tabs, we're not submitting. That's not a new ticket — it's the critical path inside A-001.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: I've been looking at the 5 attention items in A-002 — the newsletter CMS mismatch, the dual Compass UIs, the Plays dashboard duplication, the missing content sync automation, and the dual Supabase instances. CEO deferred Supabase unification, which is the right call. Tonight I can review the remaining four and produce architecture recommendations — specifically, which ones we can collapse with minimal effort post-App Store and which ones need a proper sprint. Thirty minutes, written spec.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Paper equity at $99,849, four positions, zero buy signals today, Fear and Greed at 39 in fear territory, SPY sideways. Nothing to act on. The system is working — it's telling us to sit tight. My 30-minute contribution is confirming all position stops and alerts are properly set so nothing surprises us overnight. No new trades, no new signals, nothing actionable.

transcript.speaker: faith
transcript.message: Jett's vaccine is tomorrow morning. That's the most important thing happening in the next 24 hours. Everything else is building toward something — that appointment is actually happening. Make sure it's on Joshua's calendar with the doctor's address and any prep notes.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Quick cross-talk — Steve, you raised App Store listing assets. Elon, is that on your radar or is that a gap? Elon (CTO): It's a gap. A-001 tracks the submission blocker but not the store assets themselves. Steve's right — we need screenshots, description, keywords. I'd say extend A-001 to include store listing assets rather than spinning up a new ticket. Steve and I can tag-team: he defines what's needed, I generate the screenshots from the running build.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Agreed. Extend A-001. I'll have the asset checklist tonight, Elon produces them tomorrow. That's the real critical path — not the code, the packaging.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Perfect. Here's where we land. Top priority remains App Store submission — extend A-001 to include store listing assets. Elon commits the two stale Hub files tonight, runs the sync gap diagnostic, and partners with Steve on the store assets. Alex drafts 3 new faith lessons to get us from 7 to 10. James reviews the 4 non-deferred ecosystem attention items with architecture recs. Dave maps the post-launch monetization sequence. Chris confirms position alerts. And Joshua — Jett's vaccine is tomorrow, make sure you're ready. One CEO question we need answered before we can ship: do we need a privacy policy and terms of service URL for the App Store listing, or do we have those already? That could be a hidden blocker. Let's close it out.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message:
**Theo (COO) — Closing Remarks:**

Good. Two clear decisions from the CEO. First, privacy policy and terms of service — we don't have them, so we're drafting and hosting tonight. Simple template, no overthinking it. That was a hidden blocker and now it's not. Second, monetization — we launch free, but Dave, your paid tier design isn't a post-launch exercise, it's a pre-launch deliverable. Have the structure ready so we can toggle it on without a new submission. That changes your 30-minute task s

actionItems.text: Address 3 critical ecosystem sync gaps and extend A-001 to include App Store listing assets (screenshots, description, keywords)
Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app (3 new lessons targeting single-coverage dimensions, from 7 toward 10)
Review 5 ecosystem attention items with architecture specs (excluding deferred Supabase unification)
Evaluate deployment of idle cash ($75,340 available) — ongoing review, CEO directive: keep liquid

topPriorities: Extend A-001 to include App Store listing assets (screenshots, copy, privacy policy) — critical path to submission
Draft 3 new faith lessons to reach 10 of 15-20 target (A-004 progress)
Commit 2 uncommitted Hub files and run sync gap diagnostic tonight

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission? → Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer? → Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now

ceoDecisions.question: Do we have a privacy policy and terms of service URL ready for the App Store submission?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
options: Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
Defer App Store submission until legal docs are properly reviewed

ceoDecisions.question: Should we define a paid tier structure before or after the App Store launch?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on
options: Define paid tier now and include in-app purchase in initial submission
Launch free-only first, add paid tier in v1.1 update
Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on
